name: Cannon
description_short: Cannon is not a financial services company—it is the Merriam-Webster dictionary entry for the word 'cannon,' a large gun. The website URL provided is a dictionary definition, not a company website.
description_long: Background: The provided website URL (https://www.merriam-webster.com/dictionary/cannon) points to the Merriam-Webster Dictionary entry for the word 'cannon,' not to a financial services company. The content is purely lexicographic, offering etymology, pronunciation guides, and usage examples dating back to the 15th century. There is no company information, financial services offering, or business entity present on this page. What They Offer: The page contains dictionary definitions of 'cannon' as a noun and verb, including: a large heavy gun usually mounted on a carriage; a heavy-caliber automatic aircraft gun; any device for propelling objects at high speeds; and in sports, a very strong throwing arm. The site also includes word history, synonyms, example sentences, and biographical information about Joseph Gurney Cannon (1836–1926), an American politician. What Distinguishes Them: This is not a distinguishable financial services company—it is a dictionary resource. There is no business model, no financial products, and no consumer finance operations. Honest Assessment: 'Cannon' is fundamentally miscategorized as a payday-alternative lender. The website is a reference tool, not a financial institution. No legitimate company profile can be created from this content, as there is no actual company to profile. The categorization appears to result from either a database error, a typo in the URL entry, or a misunderstanding of what company was being researched.
